description:
Symmetric counterpart to bl-4918. push_task's noop check is currently status-only:

    if stored.is_some() && last == Some(task.status.as_str()) {
        return Ok(json!({}));
    }

so a balls-side title or description edit that doesn't change status never PATCHes GH. The content mirror shipped in bl-4918 is therefore one-directional today (GH -> balls only).

bl-4918 added last_synced_title and last_synced_body_hash to the projection precisely so the push side can decide 'did balls move?'. The fix is: replace the status-only noop with a comparison against the projection — noop only when status, format!('{} [{}]', task.title, task.id), and body_hash(task.description) ALL match last_synced_*. Any one differing -> PATCH and update the projection.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Push PATCHes when balls.title changes without a status change.
- Push PATCHes when balls.description changes without a status change.
- Push noops when none of (status, title, body) moved.
- Unit test for each PATCH-vs-noop arm.
- Integration test in tests/push.rs exercising 'title-only edit -> PATCH'.
title: Push-side content mirror: PATCH GH when balls title or body moves
